title: Original intent preservation parent lane
larger_intended_outcome: Implement #1318 so broad original user intent and applicable user/system/subsystem/soft intent remain the parent acceptance target across startup, implement, Planning decomposition, child slices, proof, closeout, and final user reporting. Slices are execution mechanics, not user-level completion unless the user explicitly asks for a slice.

parent_acceptance.original_intent: Preserve full original user intent and applicable intent constraints across epics, decomposition, slices, closeout, and final response.
parent_acceptance.acceptance_target: #1318 and child issues #1319-#1325 are proven end to end, including the generated-code runtime-boundary regression and applicable-intent conflict/manual-verification fixture.
parent_acceptance.parent_proof_required: Focused tests and PR closure matrix demonstrate Planning parent acceptance, startup/implement context, closeout blocking, final response rendering, and applicable intent handling.
parent_acceptance.residual_intent_rule: If any child issue lacks proof, #1318 remains open and final response must name the residual parent intent.
parent_acceptance.clarification_needed_when: Ask or keep parent open when clear broad intent would otherwise be narrowed to a slice.

non_goals: Do not create a new project-management system or broad dashboard.
Do not add an automatic business-logic classifier or policy engine.
Do not force full Planning ceremony for small direct work.

candidate_lanes.title: Grounding review before implementation
candidate_lanes.outcome: Review existing startup, implement, Planning, Verification, closeout, and final-response surfaces before implementation.
candidate_lanes.proof: Review record names current gaps and concrete reuse points.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Provides grounding evidence for all implementation lanes.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: Implementation and proof still remain after review.

candidate_lanes.title: Capture parent original intent in startup and implement context
candidate_lanes.outcome: Startup and implement surfaces preserve the original parent intent when current work is a slice.
candidate_lanes.proof: Focused startup/implement tests show parent intent survives broad request handling.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Preserves parent intent before code changes and changed-path proof selection.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: Planning contract, closeout blocking, final response, and applicable-intent proof remain unless implemented together.

candidate_lanes.title: Parent acceptance contract in decomposition and child slices
candidate_lanes.outcome: Decomposition and active slices record original parent acceptance, slice contribution, residual parent intent, and parent closure proof.
candidate_lanes.proof: Planning schema/template/projection tests cover the parent acceptance contract.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Makes parent acceptance explicit in decomposition and child slice records.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: Parent closeout still needs blocking/final-response proof.

candidate_lanes.title: Block parent closeout on narrower proof
candidate_lanes.outcome: Closeout blocks broad parent completion when proof only covers a narrower slice.
candidate_lanes.proof: Closeout completion_options and final_response_rendering tests block parent/plain-done claims.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Prevents narrow proof from authorizing parent closure.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: Generated-code regression and final UX still need proof unless included.

candidate_lanes.title: Generated-code runtime-boundary regression fixture
candidate_lanes.outcome: A request that all runtime code be generated from IR cannot be narrowed to one fresh generated target or one primitive migration.
candidate_lanes.proof: Representative fixture asserts parent status remains open and final response must name residual runtime-boundary intent.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Proves the motivating under-scoping failure mode is blocked.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: None for #1323 if fixture passes; remaining child lanes still govern #1318.

candidate_lanes.title: Plain slice-vs-parent final response
candidate_lanes.outcome: Final user-facing closeout says what landed, what parent intent remains, what proof covers, and what must not be claimed.
candidate_lanes.proof: Final-response rendering tests cover required facts and must_not_claim constraints.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Makes parent-vs-slice status visible to the user.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: None for #1324 if rendered output passes; remaining child lanes still govern #1318.

candidate_lanes.title: Applicable intent stack and conflict handling
candidate_lanes.outcome: User, system, subsystem, soft/manual, compliance, assurance, and Verification intents are surfaced with conflict and manual-verification state.
candidate_lanes.proof: Fixture shows conflict or missing authority blocks or caveats closeout and final response cannot hide it.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Reconciles user intent with applicable system/subsystem/soft/manual obligations.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: #1326 durable source discovery remains separate unless explicitly implemented.
human_confirmation_needed: Human/domain owner accepts or clarifies conflicts and manual verification obligations.

dependency_assumptions: #1321 and #1322 should land together or in close sequence because parent acceptance without closeout blocking is weak.
#1323 should land early enough to prevent a generic wording-only fix.
#1325 is part of #1318 closure standard, while broader durable intent-source discovery in #1326 remains a sibling lane unless this PR explicitly implements it.
parallelization_assumptions: Review, Planning contract, closeout blocking, final rendering, and applicable-intent evidence can be implemented in one integrated PR only if tests prove the end-to-end path.
A child slice may close only when its proof maps back to the parent acceptance target.
proof_expectations: Startup/implement, Planning decomposition/slice, closeout_report, completion_options, final_response_rendering, Verification/applicable-intent, and generated-code runtime-boundary fixtures all pass.
Parent closure remains blocked unless the original parent intent and applicable intent stack are satisfied or explicitly routed.
promotion_rule: Promote child lanes only with explicit parent acceptance, slice contribution, residual parent intent, proof boundary, and final-response consequence.
notes: Parent acceptance target: broad original intent remains the completion target; decomposition does not authorize reducing scope. Generated-code regression target: all runtime code should be generated from IR representations as a single source of truth cannot be closed by proof that some generated target is fresh.
